payload.text:
Create a premium, minimalistic personal portfolio landing page for a Computer Science and Business Systems engineering student and fresher software developer.

DESIGN STYLE:
- Strictly black and white only (no other colors)
- Clean, modern, premium, and aesthetic
- High contrast typography
- Large white space and balanced layout
- Elegant grid system
- Subtle micro-interactions (hover underline, fade-in, smooth scroll)
- Professional and recruiter-focused
- Desktop-first and fully responsive

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Modern sans-serif font (Inter / Helvetica / SF Pro style)
- Strong hierarchy: bold headings, clean body text

--------------------------------------------------
GLOBAL NAVIGATION BEHAVIOR
--------------------------------------------------
- Fixed minimal navbar at the top
- Menu items:
  Home | About | Skills | Projects | Services | Certifications | Contact
- Clicking any navbar item should smoothly scroll to its corresponding section on the same page (anchor-based navigation with smooth scrolling)

--------------------------------------------------
HERO SECTION (Home)
--------------------------------------------------
- Full screen hero section
- Bold headline:
  "Computer Science & Business Systems Engineer | Full Stack Developer | AI Enthusiast"
- Subheadline:
  "Building scalable web applications, intelligent AI solutions, and clean user experiences."
- CTA buttons:
  [View Projects] → scrolls to Projects section
  [Contact Me] → scrolls to Contact section

--------------------------------------------------
ABOUT SECTION
--------------------------------------------------
- Professional summary:
  I am a detail-oriented Computer Science and Business Systems undergraduate with strong skills in full stack development, AI engineering, and UI/UX design. Passionate about building innovative, scalable, and sustainable digital solutions. A fast learner and collaborative team player with hands-on experience in Generative AI and Agentic AI systems.
- Education:
  Bachelor of Engineering – Computer Science & Business Systems  
  Dr. Ambedkar Institute of Technology, Bengaluru  
  CGPA: 8.9

--------------------------------------------------
SKILLS SECTION
--------------------------------------------------
Use a clean grid layout:

Languages:
- Java
- Python

Web Technologies:
- HTML
- CSS
- JavaScript
- React.js
- Node.js
- REST APIs

Databases:
- MongoDB
- MySQL
- SQL

Tools & Platforms:
- VS Code
- Figma
- Jupyter Notebook
- Google Colab
- AWS (Basics)

Core Concepts:
- Data Structures & Algorithms
- Object-Oriented Programming
- Database Design
- AI / Machine Learning

--------------------------------------------------
PROJECTS SECTION
--------------------------------------------------
Show premium project cards with title, description, and tech stack:

1. Online Library Management System
   - Automated book borrowing and inventory management
   - Tech: Node.js, MySQL, HTML, CSS, JavaScript

2. AI-Powered Healthcare Assistant
   - Health prediction using machine learning models
   - Tech: Python, Django, React.js, MongoDB, Machine Learning

3. Agentic AI Ticketing System
   - Autonomous ticket classification using LLM-powered agents
   - Tech: Python, Django, React.js, LangChain, MongoDB

4. Cloud File Sharing Application
   - Secure file sharing with JWT authentication
   - Tech: Spring Boot, React.js, AWS S3, JWT

--------------------------------------------------
SERVICES SECTION
--------------------------------------------------
Display services suitable for a fresher:

- Full Stack Web Development
- AI & Machine Learning Solutions
- REST API Development
- UI/UX Design (Figma)
- Backend Development
- Cloud-Based Application Development

--------------------------------------------------
CERTIFICATIONS & WORKSHOPS SECTION
--------------------------------------------------
- Full Stack Developer Professional Certificate (MERN Stack)
- AWS Cloud Essentials
- UX Design & Project Management (IBM)
- Generative AI Application Development Workshop
- Data Analytics Workshop
- Model Context Protocol (MCP) Workshop

--------------------------------------------------
CONTACT SECTION
--------------------------------------------------
- Minimal contact form:
  Name | Email | Message
- Interactive contact links:
  Email → opens default mail client (mailto)
  LinkedIn → https://www.linkedin.com/in/sanjush898/
  GitHub → https://github.com/HSanju22
  LeetCode → https://leetcode.com/HSanju22
- All links should open in a new tab
- Use clean black outline buttons or text links

--------------------------------------------------
FINAL CALL TO ACTION
--------------------------------------------------
- Closing line:
  "Let’s build something impactful together."
- CTA button:
  [Get in Touch] → scrolls to Contact section

--------------------------------------------------
FOOTER
--------------------------------------------------
- Minimal footer
- Text-only
- © 2026 Sanjeev S H
- Subtle divider line

Ensure smooth scrolling, anchor-based navigation, and link redirections work perfectly.
The final result should feel premium, aesthetic, modern, and highly impressive to recruiters.
 , you can only use black and white colors and related colors like grey.

**Defining the Aesthetic**

I'm currently solidifying the design principles. The vision is a stark, monochromatic portfolio. High contrast is key, with a sophisticated balance achieved through a precise grid. I'm leaning toward a modern sans-serif typeface, something in the Inter or Helvetica vein, to maintain that minimalist feel. The goal is elegance through simplicity, while remaining completely responsive.


**Detailing the Architecture**

I've been mapping out the structure, the skeleton so to speak, of the portfolio. Key sections are in place, alongside their components. The tech stack is locked in as React with TypeScript and Tailwind. The UI framework will be a responsive grid, with specific color values and type treatments. The navbar will be fixed, and the hero will have large text.


**Refining the Structure**

The layout debate is settled: centered is the route I'm taking. My focus is now on the content sections: skills will be categorized grids. Projects will be cards with subtle hover effects. Contact will have a simple form and social links. The tech stack is locked. I'm building with Inter from Google Fonts for that clean, modern touch. The underlying details, such as smooth scrolling, are falling into place.


**Considering Simplicity First**

I'm now prioritizing the requested UI/UX elements, ensuring a clean and functional design is locked down first. A "Portfolio AI" feature is tempting, but I'll stick to core requests. I'm considering subtle "fade-in" animations, ideally with simple CSS or Tailwind, keeping things lean. Also, I must ensure that the mobile menu is fully operational.

entries.description: App metadata and configuration
Entry HTML file with Google Fonts and Tailwind CSS script
React entry point
Shared type definitions
Global constants for portfolio content
Main application component with all sections
Fixed navigation bar component
FullScreen Hero section

entries.description: Minimal footer component

payload.text: Make changes, use the images as a template and make changes , should use only black, white and grey colors. add some animations.

**Refining Portfolio Design**

I'm currently working on updating the minimalist portfolio. The key focus is on refining the design to align with the provided image template. The goal is to ensure a strict adherence to a black, white, and grey color palette throughout the entire project. This means re-structuring the portfolio, too.


**Reimagining Layout Implementation**

I've been immersed in the layout's aesthetic implementation, taking inspiration from the user's screenshot. A floating navbar is emerging, complete with social icons, and I'm centering the "About Me" header. I'm prioritizing a sizable hero section and working on statistic counters to inject some dynamic flair. Service cards, client logo grids, and a detailed footer are also being sketched. Black, white, and grey form the project's essence, of course. Animations, like fade-ins and smooth transitions, will elevate it.


**Developing Final Component Suite**

I'm now fully immersed in developing the final component suite. I've started by integrating the "About Me" section, including a circular image area. Next, I am building the stats component to show the metrics. I'm also refactoring the services cards. I'm building out both the client logo grid and the footer, and I'm adding placeholder data for stats and client logos. I'll utilize Tailwind animations like `transition`, `duration`, `hover:scale`, and maybe `animate-pulse`. To replicate the glow effect from the image, I'll use soft grey gradients and shadows. Lastly, I'm updating "Sophia" to "Sanjeev".


**Adjusting Project Details**

I'm now revising the project's details. I've switched the "Brand designer" title to "Full Stack Developer" and updated the location to "Bengaluru." I'll ensure I stick to the monochromatic theme; I'll use grey for subtle accents like shadows to get that premium look, referencing the original image. I'm focusing on the structural components: navbar links, hero section, statistics, services, clients, and footer, all following the provided layout.

To transform your portfolio into the premium style shown in the template while strictly adhering to your **Black, White, and Grey** palette, I will redesign the layout with higher contrast, softer shadows, and a sophisticated grid.

### Specification of Updates:

1.  **Monochromatic Aesthetic**: I will replace all hints of color with a spectrum of greys. The background will feature subtle radial gradients in light grey to mimic the "glow" from the template without using actual colors.
2.  **Navbar & Socials**: The header will now include a functional social icon set (using SVG icons) and a cleaner, wider navigation layout.
3.  **Refined Hero/About Section**: We will combine the "About Me" and Hero into the elegant split-layout seen in the image: text and CTAs on the left, and a sophisticated circular framed image placeholder on the right.
4.  **Stats Component**: A new "Experience at a Glance" section with icons for projects, CGPA, and technical certifications.
5.  **Service Cards**: Redesigned as clean, elevated white cards with light grey borders and subtle hover lift animations.
6.  **Client/Logo Grid**: A "Technologies I Use" section styled like the "Featured Clients" grid in the template, using high-contrast black and white logos (placeholders).
7.  **Sophisticated Animations**:
    *   `Reveal-on-scroll` effects for sections.
    *   Smooth `underline-expand` on hover for nav links.
    *   Subtle `float` animation for the hero image.
8.  **Expanded Footer**: A 4-column professional footer with site map, location, and social links.

Redesign navbar to match the template style with social icons.
New split-layout hero matching the provided image template.

payload.text:
**Diagnosing Export Issues**

I'm currently trying to diagnose a compilation error in `components/Certifications.tsx`. It seems the module `../constants` isn't exporting the `CERTIFICATIONS` member, even though I've looked at the file and see a number of other exports. This will require further investigation into the declaration and export mechanism, so I can ensure it's declared and then exported properly.


**Fixing the Module Export**

I see that `CERTIFICATIONS` is missing from the `constants.tsx` export list, which is the immediate cause of the compilation error. I'll add `CERTIFICATIONS` with representative data for Sanjeev's background. Furthermore, I noticed that `About` and `Certifications` aren't used in `App.tsx` and intend to add them to make the portfolio more complete.


**Confirming Export and Usage**

I've verified the `CERTIFICATIONS` export in `constants.tsx` to resolve the immediate error. Now, it's an array of strings as needed by `Certifications.tsx`. I also double-checked for any other errors and found none. Finally, I'll integrate the `About` and `Certifications` components into `App.tsx` for full project completion.


**Reconciling Portfolio Structure**

I'm now clarifying the portfolio structure, noting `Hero.tsx` overlaps with `About.tsx`, although `About` is detailed. The navigation links in `constants.tsx` seem correctly targeting the sections. I also checked `Certifications.tsx` with its dedicated ID. Fixing the `CERTIFICATIONS` export is the priority, which will then allow the addition of the `About` and `Certifications` components to complete the project.


**Clarifying Component Order**

I'm now revising the portfolio structure. I've noted the numbering of sections, and recognized the missing section 04. I will integrate the `About` and `Certifications` components into `App.tsx` and ensure that the order is correct, as well as fixing the missing export from before. I'm focusing on the component order and numbering.


**Integrating Key Components**

I'm now focusing on integrating `About` and `Certifications` into `App.tsx`, noting that `About` must be rendered for the navigation link to function. I'll prioritize fixing `constants.tsx`, ensuring `CERTIFICATIONS` is exported, and then proceed with the component integration, checking that the `Hero` title is distinct. I've updated `constants.tsx` and will ensure a complete and consistent user experience.


**Refining the Certification Logic**

I'm now iterating over the `CERTIFICATIONS` array within `Certifications.tsx`, which relies on the export from `constants.tsx`. I need to ensure this component renders the certification data correctly, following its structure and usage in `App.tsx`. The goal is to verify data integrity within the portfolio.


**Checking for Component Issues**

I'm now investigating potential import issues in `About.tsx` and `Certifications.tsx`, noting the critical failure in `Certifications.tsx`. I'm also reconsidering the `Hero.tsx` heading which duplicates the about heading. I'll need to re-evaluate if the current "About Me" title is the best fit. I'll modify the `Hero` title if necessary, but the priority is still resolving the import. This will make `About.tsx` the official "About Me" section with `id="about"`, and ensure the navigation links work correctly with the about component. I need to make sure the changes cascade and everything works correctly. I need to check `SERVICES` as well.


**Examining Additional Exports**

I'm now focusing on checking all exports in `constants.tsx`, in addition to `CERTIFICATIONS`, to ensure nothing else is missing or incorrectly declared. I've re-examined the code and confirmed that the specified variables, `PROJECTS`, `SKILLS`, `TECH_LOGOS`, and `STATS`, are used and correctly imported into `Projects.tsx`, `Skills.tsx`, `Clients.tsx`, and `Stats.tsx`, respectively. Since all seems in order, I can now focus on the core requirement of importing the `CERTIFICATIONS` variable.


**Evaluating Remaining Imports**

I'm now methodically reviewing the other exports in `constants.tsx`, in addition to verifying the `CERTIFICATIONS` variable. I've confirmed `PROJECTS`, `SKILLS`, `TECH_LOGOS`, and `STATS` are declared and used correctly in `Projects.tsx`, `Skills.tsx`, `Clients.tsx`, and `Stats.tsx`. The recent focus has been on overall code integrity, especially in `stats.tsx`, after making several corrections.


**Reviewing Project Declarations**

I'm now going through the `constants.tsx` file and examining the `PROJECTS`, `SKILLS`, and `TECH_LOGOS` arrays to ensure everything is well-declared. I've been making sure these are well-formatted, with attention to their array and export definitions, especially after adding more entries. I'm verifying the data aligns with the overall structure.


**Examining Project Details**

I'm presently examining the `PROJECTS` array within `constants.tsx` in detail. I'm checking each project entry to confirm its structure and ensure the technology listings are accurately represented and consistent. I'm focusing on the format and data for each project, particularly the tech stacks used. I've also added services to the `constants.tsx`. The goal is data integrity and to verify the arrays.



**Checking Array Data Integrity**

I'm presently examining the `PROJECTS` array within `constants.tsx` in detail. I'm checking each project entry to confirm its structure and ensure the technology listings are accurately represented and consistent. I'm focusing on the format and data for each project, particularly the tech stacks used. I've also added services to the `constants.tsx`. The goal is data integrity and to verify the arrays.


**Validating Component Imports**

I'm now going through the `App.tsx` file to see if the `About` component has been successfully integrated, making sure there are no errors. I'll need to confirm that adding `About` in `App.tsx` allows the navigation links in the navigation bar to function correctly, in particular for the "#about" link. I'll make sure that `About` doesn't have any obvious import issues. Finally, I'll add the `Certifications` component.


**Finalizing Component Integration**

I've decided to keep the "About me" heading in `Hero.tsx` as it serves as a site title for this single-page portfolio. It's distinct from the detailed "About" section in `About.tsx`. I've confirmed `Certifications.tsx` correctly imports and uses the `CERTIFICATIONS` array. I've also verified `constants.tsx` now exports `CERTIFICATIONS`, and I've integrated the `About` and `Certifications` components into `App.tsx`, completing the planned updates.


**Adjusting Section Sequencing**

I'm now revising the portfolio's component order in `App.tsx` for logical flow and section numbering. I'm focusing on fitting `Stats`, `Services`, and `Clients` into the overall structure, specifically noting the numbering skips from 03 to 05. I'm prioritizing data flow and user experience.


**Reordering Component Hierarchy**

I'm now implementing the revised component sequence within `App.tsx` based on section numbers and logical flow. The original order had numbering gaps and structural inconsistencies, which I'm now resolving. I'm focusing on aligning `Skills` and `Projects` by their numbers, as well as checking the placement of `Services` to make sure it's consistent.


**Validating Final Dependencies**

I'm now going through the `Navbar.tsx` to double-check its imports from `constants.tsx` and confirm the correct usage of `SOCIAL_LINKS` and other variables. All appears as it should, with the imports correctly reflecting exports and usage. I'm satisfied all links and dependencies are functional.


**Checking Static Content Addition**

I'm presently examining whether the new static content I've added displays correctly in the `Stats`, `Skills`, and `Clients` sections. I'm verifying the newly added information looks correct. I'll make sure it's accurate and in the correct format.


**Reviewing Data Consistency**

I'm currently reviewing the project data within `constants.tsx` to ensure it's accurate and consistently formatted. I've rechecked the skill categories and project details. I've made sure all entries are up-to-date and correctly reflect the project's technical stacks. I'm focusing on the integrity of the data.


**Verifying Static Data**

I'm now checking the content of `SERVICES` and `SOCIAL` in `constants.tsx` to ensure their data integrity. Specifically, I'm checking the service descriptions and link formats, focusing on their completeness and correctness. All entries appear valid now, completing the data validation step.


**Verifying Social Integrations**

I'm now integrating the `SOCIAL_LINKS` object within the `Navbar` component, noting its definition in `constants.tsx`. I'll make sure the social links render correctly in the navigation bar. I'm checking that the URLs match the ones I entered to ensure they work.


**Sequencing the Application**

I'm now integrating the `Services` and `Clients` components, which were missing. I've rearranged their positioning to be in order with the project. I'm carefully reviewing the component ordering and content, and checking that the numbering scheme works.

entries.description: Add missing CERTIFICATIONS export to constants.tsx to resolve the compilation error in Certifications.tsx.
Import and integrate the About and Certifications components into the main app structure to ensure all sections are rendered correctly and match the navigation links.

payload.text:
Update the existing portfolio website with the following functional and UX improvements.
Do NOT change the visual design, typography, layout, or black & white color scheme.
Only update navigation behavior, button actions, and minor UI cleanup.

--------------------------------------------------
NAVBAR NAVIGATION (IMPORTANT)
--------------------------------------------------
- Make the navbar fully functional using anchor-based navigation.
- Clicking on the following menu items should smoothly scroll to their respective sections on the SAME page:

Home  → Hero section
About → About Me section
Services → Services section
Projects → Projects / Selected Works section
Skills → Expertise & Technologies section
Contact → Contact section

- Enable smooth scrolling animation for all navbar interactions.
- Ensure active section highlighting (optional but preferred).

--------------------------------------------------
BUTTON INTERACTIONS
--------------------------------------------------
1. "My services" button
   - Redirect to a separate dedicated Services page (e.g., /services)
   - If a new page is not supported, scroll to the Services section as fallback.

2. "Let's talk now" button
   - Redirect to a separate Contact page (e.g., /contact)
   - If separate page is not supported, scroll to the Contact section.

3. "View case study" buttons
   - Prepare them for future project detail pages.
   - For now, allow smooth scroll to the Projects section or open placeholder project pages.

--------------------------------------------------
REMOVE UNNECESSARY ELEMENTS
--------------------------------------------------
- Remove the "LIVE PORTFOLIO" badge/text from the top section.
- Keep the hero section clean, premium, and distraction-free.

--------------------------------------------------
CONTACT SECTION LINK BEHAVIOR
--------------------------------------------------
- Email should be clickable using mailto:
  shsanjeev321@gmail.com

- Social links should redirect correctly and open in a new tab:
  LinkedIn → https://www.linkedin.com/in/sanjush898/
  GitHub   → https://github.com/HSanju22
  LeetCode → https://leetcode.com/HSanju22

--------------------------------------------------
CTA SECTION
--------------------------------------------------
- "Get in Touch" button should scroll to the Contact section.
- Ensure consistent behavior across all CTA buttons.

--------------------------------------------------
GENERAL UX REQUIREMENTS
--------------------------------------------------
- Maintain strict black & white theme.
- Keep minimalistic, premium aesthetic.
- Ensure all navigation and buttons work smoothly and consistently.
- Do not add new colors or visual effects beyond subtle transitions.

Apply only these updates and preserve the existing design integrity.

**Focusing Website Updates**

I'm currently thinking about improving my portfolio website's functionality and user experience. I want to add some features but without deviating from its black-and-white minimalist design. I am considering updating the "Home" section to be a Hero section, using the `#home` fragment identifier for the anchor tag.


**Implementing Navigation Links**

I've been mapping out the internal links for the website, ensuring a smooth user flow. I'm focusing on the anchor links and button behaviors now. Specifically, I'm integrating scroll-to functionality for sections like 'Services' and 'Contact' using specific fragment identifiers. I have removed the "LIVE PORTFOLIO" badge in the Hero section, and I'm updating my `constants.tsx` and `components/Hero.tsx` files. I am also working on external links, making them open in new tabs and have updated my email.


**Refining Section Anchors**

I'm now implementing the anchor link updates across the site. I've ensured the `About`, `Skills`, and `Services` sections have their respective `id` attributes. Also, I've updated the links in `Hero.tsx` to correctly point to `#services` and `#contact`. In `Contact.tsx`, I am working on the email text, and changing the contact link. I've also updated the email `constants.tsx`, and I'm deciding what to do with the "View Case Study" action in `Projects.tsx`.


**Updating Contact Details**

I just corrected the email address in `constants.tsx` to `shsanjeev321@gmail.com`. I've verified that the "My Services" link, as well as "Let's Talk Now" buttons in the Hero section, will navigate correctly using anchor links within the single-page application. I also ensured the "View Case Study" action in `Projects.tsx` points to `#projects`. I believe all the navigation elements and contact section links are properly configured and tested.


**Validating Site Anchors**

I've been meticulously cross-checking the anchor links. I've updated the email in `Contact.tsx` and ensured the "View Case Study" action smoothly scrolls to the projects section. The component IDs like `#projects`, `#skills`, `#about`, etc., are all correctly implemented and verified across components. Now, I feel confident that all navigation elements function as intended.


**Confirming Contact Updates**

I'm verifying that the email address in `constants.tsx` and `Contact.tsx` are correctly updated to `shsanjeev321@gmail.com`. I've confirmed that all anchor links, including those for the "Projects," "Skills," and "About" sections, are accurately pointing to their respective IDs. I've also validated the navigation consistency across the entire site. I am now moving on.

Remove LIVE PORTFOLIO badge and ensure button interactions scroll correctly to their sections.

--------------------------------------------------

1. "My services" button

Apply only these updates and preserve the existing design integrity.

**Considering Portfolio Enhancements**

I'm currently focused on the functional and UX upgrades for my portfolio website. I'm keeping the visual design, color scheme, and layout consistent. My priority is to identify and implement targeted improvements to user experience and functionality.


**Optimizing User Flow**

I've implemented fully functional anchor-based navigation for all core sections of the portfolio. Smooth scrolling is working as expected. I'm prioritizing active section highlighting for improved visual feedback. Button interactions, such as "My Services" and "Let's Talk," now smoothly scroll to their respective sections, as a fallback, given current limitations. I've also verified the removal of the "LIVE PORTFOLIO" badge.


**Finalizing UX Refinements**

I'm now implementing the final UX details. I've ensured all internal links, like "My Services" and "Get in Touch", scroll smoothly to the correct sections, using their `#id` anchors. External links, such as social media and email, open in new tabs as expected. The minimalistic black and white theme with smooth transitions is holding up consistently across the design.


**Prioritizing Interactive Updates**

I'm now prioritizing the implementation of active section highlighting in the Navbar using an IntersectionObserver. I'll make sure to verify the link correctness across all sections: Hero, Footer, and Contact components. My goal is to add dynamic visual feedback and a fully functional UI. I'll ensure the removal of the badge from previous updates stays consistent.


**Validating Section Anchors**

I'm now cross-referencing all anchor links to ensure they accurately match their corresponding section IDs. This includes verifying the smooth scrolling behavior across the site. I'm focusing on `Navbar.tsx` and `Footer.tsx` for potential discrepancies. I've located placeholder text and hardcoded links that require updating in the footer, such as the contact email and phone number.


**Implementing Active Navigation**

I'm now focusing on implementing the active section highlighting in the navigation bar using an IntersectionObserver, as planned. I'm taking the anchor validation from previous updates, and integrating dynamic visual feedback into the Navbar. I'll maintain the existing design, ensuring the active link visually indicates the current section. I'll stick to a minimalistic approach for now, focusing on the core functional improvements.


**Confirming Link Integrity**

I'm verifying that the implementation of active section highlighting in the navigation bar using the IntersectionObserver is working correctly. I've double-checked all anchor links and confirmed they accurately match their corresponding section IDs. This includes verifying the smooth scrolling behavior across the site. I've also confirmed that links such as "#home" are working as expected. I've updated placeholder values in the footer, such as the contact email, ensuring consistency.


**Applying Consistent Theme**

I'm now focusing on ensuring consistent visual design across the portfolio, as I recall the user wants a "Strict black, white, and grey" theme. While the subtle gradient looks premium, I'll remove it. I'll also update my email in `Footer.tsx` from `example.com` to `shsanjeev321@gmail.com`. I'll ensure no badge is present in the `Hero.tsx`. My current focus is implementing the planned updates in Navbar and Footer.


**Developing Mobile Navigation**

I'm now integrating mobile menu functionality into the `Navbar.tsx` component. I'll focus on adding the logic for the hamburger button to toggle the menu. Given the focus on navigation, I'll avoid changing the visual design and layout, adhering to the user's instructions. I'll verify the updated links.

entries.description: Implemented active section highlighting using IntersectionObserver and added mobile menu toggle logic for better navigation behavior.
Corrected the hardcoded email address and phone number placeholder to reflect professional standards and user requirements.

payload.text:
**Examining Past Revisions**

I'm now diving into the file history. My objective is to pinpoint the two most recent alterations. It looks like the initial version was a minimalistic portfolio. The first change involved a switch to a black/white/grey theme inspired by a "Sophia" template. This includes a Hero layout alteration, monochromatic color scheme, and incorporation of Stats and Clients sections. Now I need to identify the second change!


**Determining User Intent**

I'm now clarifying the user's intent concerning "undo the last two changes." Initially, I assumed the user wanted to revert to an older file version. Now, I'm considering two other interpretations: either the two most recent messages I sent, or the two major architectural shifts in the project. My current focus is to determine the most logical interpretation for "two changes" in this context. It seems like the best approach is to roll back to the state *before* the UX/functional improvements, and before the Sophia-inspired update.


**Analyzing Target Reversion**

I've determined that "undoing the last two changes" means reverting to the original portfolio state. I'm focusing on the code differences between the original and the "Sophia" template request. This involves the Hero section change, and the light grey bg cards, the black cards with white border (Projects), icon metrics (Stats), and tech logo grid (Clients) additions. My goal is to arrive at the original minimalistic portfolio.


**Recalling the Original Design**

I'm now reconstructing the original state of the portfolio. This involves recalling the layout and component structure. I'm focusing on the `App.tsx` file and the centered Hero section. It was monochromatic but distinct from the "Sophia" design, with a premium-minimalistic feel, pre-UX upgrades. I also need to remove the UX improvements: the active nav, specific links and the removed badge. The "Sophia" design changes include the split hero, stats, and clients components and associated theme logic.


**Defining Target State**

I'm now clarifying the specifics of the reversion, based on recent thoughts. I think the user is requesting to undo the two most recent *major* updates. That means, I'll need to remove the UI elements added from the prompt: `Clients.tsx`, `Stats.tsx`, and the split-layout `Hero.tsx`. I should also revert `Navbar.tsx` to its initial, simpler version. Lastly, I'll return the `Hero.tsx` component to its original centered layout with the title "Computer Science & Business Systems Engineer". My focus is now on those components.


**Updating Specific Files**

I'm now methodically addressing the components. The `constants.tsx` and `App.tsx` files are being modified to reflect the state of reversion. I'm removing the `STATS`, `TECH_LOGOS` and `Clients` and `Stats` components. Additionally, I will now be modifying `Hero.tsx` for centered text. I also plan to modify `Navbar.tsx` for a simple, fixed navigation bar, and will modify `index.html` to be a simple white background without any complex design or reveal logic.


**Recalling User's Requests**

I'm revisiting the user's initial instructions to solidify the target state for reversion. It appears I've overcomplicated things. The user wants the original minimalist design, as the "visual design" was NOT to be changed by me in the last version. I will provide the original, minimalistic portfolio, and start modifying those files. I need to make sure I *only* remove my changes from the "Sophia" design, and go back to the original design. I will check file history for that first prompt now.



**Finalizing Reversion Logic**

I've determined I need to discard the state from the UX tweaks, and the Sophia template. I'm focusing on reconstructing the components that reflect the original, minimalist design. The original design has a simple Navbar with lowercase links, a centered Hero with the specified title, and a straightforward Projects grid alongside a Skills list. The target state means the omission of the Stats and Clients sections, alongside any more complex logic from my previous attempts. My focus now is on confirming the files, and getting the output finalized.

entries.description: Revert to the original minimalist layout and background styles, removing the Sophia-template gradients and scroll reveal logic.
Revert constants to the original set, removing the Sophia-inspired stats and tech logos.
Revert App structure to remove Stats and Clients sections and restore the original minimalist order.
Revert navbar to original design with simple links and mobile menu toggle, removing intersection observer logic.
Revert Hero to the original centered minimalist design.

**Addressing Missing Constants**

I'm focusing on the missing constants in `constants.tsx`, specifically `STATS`. I'll start by examining this file to identify where `STATS` should be defined and how it relates to other constants. Then, I plan to integrate the required data within `STATS` to resolve the errors in `components/Stats.tsx` and `components/Clients.tsx`.


**Adding Type Declarations**

I've decided to add the `Stat` and `TechLogo` interfaces to `types.ts`, which already exists, to ensure type safety. I'm focusing now on defining `STATS` and `TECH_LOGOS` directly in `constants.tsx` as the source of truth, considering the existing structure and constraints, and avoiding creating unnecessary files. For each stat, I'm integrating the corresponding SVG icons for visual representation. The aim is to make sure those constants exist in `constants.tsx`, and that they are correctly typed. I also confirmed that the `icon` property of `Stat` needs to contain the SVG path string.

entries.description: Added Stat and TechLogo interfaces to define data structures for the Stats and Clients components.
